message:
docs(readme): use new Scalar From .into() ergonomics in Quick Start + case_when

fd90.183 (frankenpandas-4m90b): with fd90.182 having added From<bool>,
From<i64>, From<f64>, From<&str>, From<String> for Scalar, update the
documented examples to use the cleaner .into() pattern instead of explicit
Scalar::Utf8(...) wrapping.

- README.md:222 (Quick Start): drop Scalar::Utf8 wrapper from date string
- README.md:1096-1097 (case_when): drop Scalar::Utf8 wrapper from "A"/"B"
- tests/readme_quick_example.rs:60: mirror the Quick Start update so the
  integration test continues to track the documented surface

Other Scalar::Utf8 occurrences (categorical, isin, etc.) left as-is —
in scope for fd90.183 was only the two examples flagged in the bead.

Verified: 22 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests all pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -120,6 +120,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-4hkt","title":"fp-conformance: live pandas oracle for DataFrameGroupBy.rolling min|max|count","description":"Add mock-free fp-conformance coverage against the live pandas oracle for DataFrameGroupBy.rolling(window).min(), max(), and count(). Use interleaved groups plus missing values so windows prove group-local scoping and null-count semantics. Capture parity through phase2c artifacts instead of fp-frame-only unit tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-22T05:29:31.198884127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T07:22:53.966730221Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T07:22:53.964890760Z","close_reason":"shipped 3269824: 2 fp-frame tests (groupby_rolling_min_max_count_int64_input_widens_to_float, groupby_rolling_window_one_emits_per_row_identity) complementary to x5oy's Float64 window=2 baseline. Locks Int64->Float64 dtype widening on rolling aggregations + window=1 degenerate identity invariant. Clippy clean; 8/8 groupby_rolling tests green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing"]}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-4m90b","title":"fd90.183: README Quick Start + case_when use new Scalar From .into() ergonomics","description":"fd90.74-182 follow-on. fd90.182 added From<bool/i64/f64/&str/String> impls for Scalar. Update the README's Quick Start example to use the new ergonomic .into() pattern so readers see the cleaner syntax instead of the explicit Scalar::Utf8(...) form.

Before (line 222):
  let dates = Series::from_values("d", vec![0i64.into()], vec![Scalar::Utf8("2024-01-15".into())])?;

After (cleaner via fd90.182):
  let dates = Series::from_values("d", vec![0i64.into()], vec!["2024-01-15".into()])?;

Also updates the case_when example (lines 1086-1090) where Vec<Scalar> values are constructed via repeat for "Good"/"Poor"/"Excellent" — the inner Scalar::Utf8(...) wrappers can become "Good".into() etc.

Pure README change — fd90.182 already shipped the From impls.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T18:08:36.244959583Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T18:12:33.417764029Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T18:12:33.415681515Z","close_reason":"Updated README Quick Start (line 222) and case_when example (lines 1086-1090) to use the new From<&str>/From<i64>/etc ergonomics introduced by fd90.182. Mirrored the same change in tests/readme_quick_example.rs:60. All 22 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-4r56","title":"[MEDIUM] parity: Timedelta arithmetic + TZ-aware datetime ops","description":"Parity-gap audit: datetime + Timedelta arithmetic, TZ-aware operations.

**Scope.** pandas:
- Timestamp + Timedelta → Timestamp.
- Timestamp - Timestamp → Timedelta.
- Timedelta + Timedelta → Timedelta.
- Timedelta * scalar → Timedelta.
- tz-aware: `pd.Timestamp('2024-01-01', tz='US/Eastern') + pd.Timedelta(days=1)` stays in the same tz.
- DST transitions: adding 1 day across a spring-forward boundary changes wall-clock hour but preserves absolute duration.

**FrankenPandas state.** fp-types has `Timedelta` namespace with NANOS_PER_* constants + parse(). No Timestamp type surfaced publicly; datetime values live as Utf8-encoded ISO 8601 strings per README Limitations.

Gaps (from README + existing DISCREPANCIES):
- No native Timestamp dtype → TZ-aware arithmetic not possible.
- Timedelta arithmetic: `Timedelta + Timedelta` not exposed as a pub fn; only internals.
- Scalar::Timedelta64 exists but no `Scalar::Timestamp` / `Scalar::DatetimeTz`.

**Severity.** MEDIUM — datetime handling is pandas's foundational surface; partial coverage limits financial/time-series workflows. chrono-tz is already a workspace dep per Cargo.toml inspection, so IANA tz data is available.

**Fix shape.** Phased:
- Phase 1: expose Timedelta + Timedelta arithmetic as pub fns in fp-types.
- Phase 2: add Timestamp struct (i64 nanos since Unix epoch, optional chrono_tz::Tz).
- Phase 3: live-oracle fixtures for dt arithmetic: Timestamp + Timedelta, Timestamp - Timestamp, tz-aware shift across DST, leap-second handling.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T02:12:19.958740286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T02:20:08.283613382Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T02:20:08.281014037Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["datetime","parity-gap","review","timedelta","tz"]}
